entries.title: The AI Hire Doesn’t Answer to Anyone
entries.summary: Companies have spent decades organizing around the limits of human coordination. Departments, reporting lines and job titles exist because complexity had to be made manageable. Artificial intelligence agents are now challenging that architecture — not as tools that sit beneath the org chart, but as participants that some companies are placing directly on it. According […] The post The AI Hir

entries.title: OpenAI Launches ChatGPT Agent That Executes Complex Workflows
entries.summary: OpenAI has begun rolling out a new agent in ChatGPT that is designed for work that is more complex than a typical chat request. The new ChatGPT Work can take action across the user’s apps and files; can spend hours on a project, if needed; and can produce sheets, slides, docs, web apps and other […] The post OpenAI Launches ChatGPT Agent That Executes Complex Workflows appeared first on PYMN

entries.title: An AI Told Millions Norway Won. The Match Hadn’t Started
entries.summary: Every four years, the World Cup tests the world’s best soccer players. In 2026, it is also testing AI. Microsoft Copilot, Google Gemini and other AI models are being asked to pick the champion. Opta’s supercomputer is simulating the tournament 25,000 times per round to estimate each team’s odds. entries.title: Axos Aims to Expand AI-Powered Financial Services With Arc Acquisition
entries.summary: Axos Financial subsidiary Axos Nevada Holding plans to acquire Arc Technologies to expand Axos’ artificial intelligence capabilities and enhance its ability to serve small businesses across the United States. The companies have entered into a definitive agreement for the acquisition and expect the transaction to close this month, subject to customary closing conditions, Axos Financial […] Th

entries.title: AI investing startup GIM raises $20 million
entries.summary: Grace Investment Machine (GIM) has raised $20 million in Series A funding to build agentic systems for capital markets.

entries.title: European Parliament backs digital euro, paving way for final negotiations
entries.summary: The European Parliament has voted in favour of the creation of a digital euro, paving the way for the final stage of negotiations with EU state members on the makeup of the CBDC ahead of a potential 2029 launch.

entries.title: Emerging Payments Technologies: What Will the Direction of Travel Look Like?
entries.summary: Joining the FinextraTV Studio at EBAday 2026 in Copenhagen, Daniel Szmukler, Director, Euro Banking Association (EBA) spoke about recently released papers on emerging technologies and their impact on the future of payments. Specifically, Szmukler discusses the direction of travel for agentic AI and for tokenised money and how they will change payment processes, customer experience and the payments

entries.title: ABN Amro punished for AML shortcomings
entries.summary: ABN AMRO has taken note of the decision by De Nederlandsche Bank (DNB) to impose an administrative fine of EUR 8.5 million in relation to structural shortcomings in the execution of due diligence for a part of its high-risk customers.

entries.title: Nodu receives MiCA and Payment Institution licences from the Bank of Latvia
entries.summary: Nodu, the stablecoin payment infrastructure company, has been granted two licences by Latvijas Banka (the Bank of Latvia): a Crypto-Asset Service Provider (CASP) licence under the EU's Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation (MiCA), and a payment institution licence.

entries.title: CaixaBank builds public benches out of recycled bank cards
entries.summary: CaixaBank has embarked on a novel recycling project, turning old bank cards into benches that will sit in villages where the lender's mobile branches visit.

entries.title: Block reaches $45m Cash App fraud probe settlement with US states
entries.summary: Block has reached a $45 million settlement with 46 US states over allegations that the firm's Cash App peer-to-peer payments app failed to protect users from fraud.

entries.title: Debit cards top payments, Fed says
entries.summary: While the cards still dominate payments, growth in their use for 2024 was the slowest since a triennial Federal Reserve report started in 2001.

entries.title: Aria raises €7m and launches €240m debt facility to scale invoice financing
entries.summary: Parisian embedded invoice financing platform Aria has raised €7 million in a Series A extension and launched a €240 million debt facility to scale its capacity.

entries.title: IPC connects with Luware's cloud-based compliance recording tool
entries.summary: IPC Systems today announced a strategic partnership with Luware that expands access to Luware Recording, a cloud-based compliance recording platform for financial institutions.

entries.title: Cash App triggers $45M settlement
entries.summary: Block misled consumers about the safety of using the financial app and failed to help users who were fraud victims, 46 states alleged.

entries.title: U.S. Bank pursues payments-first strategy
entries.summary: With payments as the entry point to banking relationships, the lender cultivates a low-cost funding base, said executive Arijit Roy.
